parentQuestion: Which of the following connective tissues provides the framework for lymphoid or...

options: Organs involved in immune system function
scaffolds.explain: Lymphoid organs (like lymph nodes, spleen, thymus, bone marrow) are part of the immune system. They house immune cells and coordinate the body's defense against infection. we'll explore their architecture together.

scaffolds.q: A delicate mesh or network structure inside an organ is called the:
scaffolds.explain: The stroma is the supportive framework of an organ — like scaffolding in a building. The parenchyma is the functional cells that do the organ's main work. Architecture matters, darling — form creates function.
